workflows.name: Plan and Code
workflows.description: Design a solution then implement it with code review

steps.prompt_template: Analyze this task and create a technical design plan with architecture decisions, data structures, and implementation steps: {input}

Based on this design plan, write the complete implementation with error handling and comments:

{design_plan}

Review this code for bugs, edge cases, and improvements. List issues found and suggest fixes:

{implementation}

workflows.name: Research and Write
workflows.description: Research a topic thoroughly then produce a written deliverable

steps.prompt_template: Research this topic thoroughly. Provide key facts, different perspectives, and source types you would cite: {input}

Using these research notes, write a clear, well-structured article. Use headings, be concise, cite the research:

{research_notes}

workflows.name: Debug Pipeline
workflows.description: Diagnose a bug then verify the fix with tests

steps.prompt_template:
Analyze this bug report. Identify the likely root cause, explain why it happens, and propose a fix:

{input}

Write the code fix based on this diagnosis. Include the corrected code and explain what changed:

{diagnosis}

workflows.name: Content Pipeline
workflows.description: Draft content then edit for quality and consistency

steps.prompt_template: Write a first draft for this content request. Focus on getting the ideas down clearly: {input}

Edit this draft for clarity, conciseness, tone consistency, and factual accuracy. The voice should be warm, direct, and grounded. Return the improved version:

{draft}

workflows.name: Security Audit
workflows.description: Scan for vulnerabilities then review findings

steps.prompt_template:
Perform a security audit of this code or configuration. Check for: injection, auth issues, secrets exposure, misconfigurations, and OWASP Top 10 risks:

{input}

Prioritize these security findings by severity (critical/high/medium/low). For each, provide a remediation step:

{findings}

Write code fixes for these prioritized security findings. Focus on critical and high severity items first:

{prioritized_findings}

workflows.name: Onboarding Guide
workflows.description: Create a beginner-friendly guide with support follow-up

steps.prompt_template: Create a step-by-step getting started guide for a complete beginner on this topic. Use simple language, analogies, and check understanding at each step: {input}

Based on this guide, anticipate the top 5 questions a beginner would ask and write clear answers for each:

{guide}
